parsed_args.prompt: In {{spacex_s1}}, find all references to Terafab and quote them verbatim. Specifically look for: (1) the complete glossary definition of Terafab including the full sentence; (2) any specific dollar amounts, capital expenditure figures, or funding commitments tied to Terafab; (3) any specific dates, years, or milestones for Terafab completion; (4) any cost-per-watt, cost-per-chip, or unit economics figures; (5) the exact language describing the Tesla and Intel collaboration framework; (6) any risk factor language specifically about Terafab.

tool_output.output:
Based on the provided text, here are all the verbatim references to Terafab, organized by your specific requests:

**(1) The complete glossary definition of Terafab including the full sentence**
*   "• “Terafab” refers to a chip manufacturing initiative with a long-term goal of producing one terawatt of compute" *(Note: The text cuts off here in the provided chunk, but this is the complete verbatim sentence fragment provided).*

**(2) Specific dollar amounts, capital expenditure figures, or funding commitments tied to Terafab**
There are no specific dollar amounts or funding commitments detailed in the text. The only mention of capital expenditures states that they are currently undetermined:
*   "Any specific projects undertaken pursuant to this framework will be subject to separate negotiations and agreements (including any development timelines, milestones and capital expenditures) and have not yet been determined."

**(3) Specific dates, years, or milestones for Terafab completion**
*   "We announced a collaboration with Tesla in March 2026 to build the Terafab initiative with a long-term goal of producing one terawatt of compute hardware each year."
*   "Intel joined the project in April 2026 and is expected to contribute its expertise in designing, fabricating, and packaging ultra-high performance chips to help Terafab scale."
*   "Intel, which has the ability to design, fabricate, and package ultra-high-performance chips at scale, also joined the Terafab project in early April 2026."
*   "We expect Terafab to be the world’s largest chip manufacturing facility, with the goal of eventually achieving one terawatt of annual compute production capacity."
*   "Our strategy for Terafab is to vertically integrate across the design of lithography masks, fabrication of logic and memory chips, and design of advanced packaging in a single closed-loop plant. Conducting all these activities end-to-end in a single facility enables rapid testing and iterations, allowing us to improve chip design and scale manufacturing faster. We expect that our speed and cost advantage from vertical integration will allow us to scale efficiently in AI chip manufacturing towards our long-term goal of producing one terawatt of compute each year."

**(4) Cost-per-watt, cost-per-chip, or unit economics figures**
There are no specific cost-per-watt, cost-per-chip, or unit economics figures provided in the text. The document only makes general claims about cost advantages:
*   "We expect that our speed and cost advantage from vertical integration will allow us to scale efficiently in AI chip manufacturing."
*   "Terafab is designed to create powerful ecosystem synergies that accelerate innovation cycles and reduce costs."
*   "we expect significant speed and cost advantages from Terafab’s vertical integration."
*   "The addition of Terafab, an initiative together with Tesla to build a manufacturing facility capable of producing 1 terawatt per year of compute hardware, aims to further extend our vertical integration to chip design and manufacturing to alleviate potential future chip shortages at SpaceX, optimize compute performance, and potentially reduce overall compute costs."

**(5) The exact language describing the Tesla and Intel collaboration framework**
*   "In connection with such collaboration, we have agreed with Tesla on a general framework for the future development of Terafab. Intel joined the project in April 2026 and is expected to contribute its expertise in designing, fabricating, and packaging ultra-high performance chips to help Terafab scale. Any specific projects undertaken pursuant to this framework will be subject to separate negotiations and agreements (including any development timelines, milestones and capital expenditures) and have not yet been determined."
*   "We plan to deepen our strategic collaboration with Tesla and Intel through Terafab."
*   "Leveraging shared engineering resources, intellectual property, and infrastructure across Tesla and SpaceX, as well as Intel’s expertise in designing, fabricating and packaging ultra-high-performance chips at scale, Terafab is designed to create powerful ecosystem synergies that accelerate innovation cycles and reduce costs."
*   "Through our Terafab initiative together with Tesla and Intel, we intend to further extend our vertical integration to chip design and manufacturing to alleviate potential future chip shortages at SpaceX, optimize compute performance, and reduce overall compute costs. Intel will contribute its expertise in designing, fabricating, and packaging ultra-high-performance chips to help Terafab scale."
*   "We are partnering to build Terafab in order to support growth in two kinds of chips— one type optimized for terrestrial edge and inference to be used primarily in Tesla’s Optimus robots and vehicles, and another type optimized for the space environment to be used in our orbital compute infrastructure."

**(6) Risk factor language specifically about Terafab**
*   "While Terafab is intended to expand our internal chip manufacturing capabilities and alleviate potential future AI chip shortages at SpaceX, particularly as we pursue orbital AI at scale, we expect to continue sourcing a significant portion of our compute hardware from third-party suppliers, and there can be no assurance that we will be able to achieve our objectives with respect to Terafab within the expected timeframes, or at all. While we have a framework agreement with Tesla, neither Tesla nor Intel are obligated to remain a part of the project, and we may not enter into any such definitive agreements."
